name: wtf-commit
description: AI commit messages from your git diff. Conventional Commits, your own API key — DeepSeek, Gemini, OpenAI, and more.

wtfCommit.commitMessageLanguage.markdownDescription: **Commit Message Language** — Language for the generated commit message (not the extension UI). When set to `Custom`, also set `wtfCommit.customCommitMessageLanguage` in `settings.json` (e.g. `French`, `Emoji only`).

wtfCommit.provider.markdownDescription: **Provider** — Default is **DeepSeek** (`deepseek-v4-flash`). Use **OpenRouter** or **Custom** for other OpenAI-compatible endpoints (MiMo, GLM, Z.AI, NVIDIA NIM, proxies). Leave Model empty for built-in defaults.

wtfCommit.autoCommit.markdownDescription: **Auto Commit** (default on): Commit after generate. Turn off to only fill Source Control. **Auto Push** only runs when this is on.

wtfCommit.autoPush.markdownDescription: **Auto Push** (default off): After auto-commit, push to the remote. Requires **Auto Commit**. Use **Confirm Before Push** as a safety gate.

wtfCommit.confirmAutoPush.markdownDescription: **Confirm Before Push** (default on): Ask before pushing when Auto Push is enabled. Only applies when Auto Push is on.

wtfCommit.showStatusBarItem.description: Show the WTF Commit icon in the status bar.

wtfCommit.baseUrl.description: API Base URL for the Custom provider only.
wtfCommit.baseUrl.markdownDescription: **Custom only.** API Base URL when **Provider** is `Custom`. Built-in providers ignore this — use **Provider Overrides** to change their endpoint/model.

wtfCommit.model.description: Model name for the Custom provider only.
wtfCommit.model.markdownDescription: **Custom only.** Model name when **Provider** is `Custom`. Built-in providers ignore this — leave empty for the provider default, or use **Provider Overrides**.

wtfCommit.ignorePaths.markdownDescription: Extra file or directory name patterns to exclude from the diff sent to the AI. Each entry is matched against a path segment (for a bare name like `generated`) or as a filename suffix (for an entry starting with `.` or `*`, e.g. `*.snap`, `.gen.ts`). Matches are case-insensitive. Built-in filters (lock files, build outputs, binaries, etc.) always apply. Set to `[]` to disable these defaults.

scripts.pretest: pnpm run compile && pnpm run lint && pnpm run docs:providers:check
scripts.package: pnpm run compile && pnpm run docs:providers:check && pnpm run package:audit && vsce package
scripts.package:vscode: pnpm run compile && node scripts/run-with-publisher.mjs CoderTesla -- pnpm exec vsce package
scripts.publish:ovsx: PAT=${OVSX_PAT:-}; if [ -z "$PAT" ] && [ "$(uname -s)" = "Darwin" ] && command -v security >/dev/null 2>&1; then PAT=$(security find-generic-password -a "$USER" -s "wtf-commit.ovsx" -w 2>/dev/null || true); fi; if [ -z "$PAT" ]; then echo "Open VSX token not found." >&2; echo "Set OVSX_PAT or save it in macOS Keychain with service 'wtf-commit.ovsx'." >&2; exit 1; fi; pnpm exec ovsx publish "wtf-commit-${npm_package_version}.vsix" --pat "$PAT"
scripts.publish:vscode: PAT=${VSCE_PAT:-}; if [ -z "$PAT" ] && [ "$(uname -s)" = "Darwin" ] && command -v security >/dev/null 2>&1; then PAT=$(security find-generic-password -a "$USER" -s "wtf-commit.vsce" -w 2>/dev/null || true); fi; if [ -z "$PAT" ]; then echo "Visual Studio Marketplace token not found." >&2; echo "Set VSCE_PAT or save it in macOS Keychain with service 'wtf-commit.vsce'." >&2; echo "See UPDATEFLOW.md for PAT setup (Marketplace Manage scope)." >&2; exit 1; fi; pnpm run compile && node scripts/run-with-publisher.mjs CoderTesla -- pnpm exec vsce publish --pat "$PAT"
scripts.release: pnpm run package && pnpm run publish:ovsx && pnpm run clean:vscode-test
scripts.release:all: pnpm run package && pnpm run publish:ovsx && pnpm run publish:vscode && pnpm run clean:vscode-test
